# ID-31: Canonical-pipeline → task-list migration approach (Linear-style umbrella/tag model)

Migrate the canonical-pipeline implementation plan (docs/specs/canonical-pipeline-implementation-plan/PLAN.md, T1-T14) into task-list.json as top-level Tasks with the PLAN doc remaining as spec substrate (Option 2 per S62 W2 Liam ratification). Includes retrospective Task population for T1-T6 + pre-phase work already shipped, and introduces an umbrella/tag model for grouping Tasks under multi-stream initiatives (Linear Initiative analogue).

Motivation: Per s48-feedback.md line 200: "Items on KH backlog and roadmap are essentially 'tasks', which when moved to the task-list, will be decomposed to subtasks." The current canonical-pipeline PLAN.md is rich substrate but lives outside the task-list, breaking traceability/observability of the single canonical work surface. S61 WP7 incorrectly scoped canonical-pipeline as out-of-scope for the production-readiness task-list — user intent is migration into one global task-list.

Approach (per Liam ratification): (1) preserve PLAN.md as spec substrate (auto-syncs to Astro docs site via ID-9); (2) open one Task per Tn with cross_doc_links + slim description; (3) retrospectively encode T1-T6 + pre-phase work as `done` Tasks with provenance journal blocks citing commits + sessions; (4) introduce umbrella/tag model (Option C: separate umbrellas.json document with curated task_ids[] per umbrella + substrate_doc field) for project-plan-as-tag concept.

## Acceptance criteria

- 14 Tasks opened per T1-T14 mapping (Task IDs renumber depending on Liam ratification of WP5 §3 proposed IDs).
- 6 retrospective Tasks (T1-T6) populated with `done` status + `<info added on …>` journal blocks citing commits + main-track session refs.
- docs/reference/umbrellas.json created with at minimum one umbrella `canonical-pipeline-implementation` listing the 14 task_ids + substrate_doc reference to PLAN.md.
- Cross-branch task-list divergence resolved at next merge cycle (stale-merge only per RESEARCH §6).
- PLAN.md retains role as spec substrate; Task descriptions reference PLAN.md sections via cross_doc_links.

## Dependencies

ID-9 (Astro docs) for auto-sync wiring; ID-30 (consolidation) if roadmap-source items also migrate. RESEARCH (31.1) is parallel-eligible with both — done.

**S67 W1c close-out — Wave 1 of ID-31 COMPLETE.** All 11 Subtasks (31.1-31.11) `done`. Subtask 31.7 added canonical-pipeline + 3 stub umbrellas to umbrellas.json; 31.8 added round-trip + cross-doc tests (76/76 PASS); 31.9 surgically extended update-roadmap-backlog skill (Create-mode task-list target + umbrella_id field + Step 8 umbrella membership); 31.10 appended MAX-ID cross-branch discipline to workflow-orchestration SKILL.md; 31.11 smoke gate green. Wave 3 retrospective T1-T6 retro Task opens pending — dispatched separately by future Orchestrator session via update-roadmap-backlog Create mode (task-list target + umbrella_id='canonical-pipeline').

Close-out gates: all per-Subtask Checker verdicts PASS or PASS_WITH_NOTES with promote=true; OOS findings triaged orch-direct (backlog ID-86 added for doc-freshness gap); 4 other findings disposed as no-action (informational/expected per spec). End-of-task code-simplification + quality-review Executor/Checker passes NOT invoked — Task scope was schema + data + tests + skill body (no substantial code surface warranting separate simplification pass).

- §3 T1-T14 → Task ID mapping (proposed): T1→ID-30 (now ID-33+ pending renumber since ID-30 took by this Task chain), T2→ID-31 (renumber), T3→ID-32 (renumber), …, T8 anchored at ID-28 (already open on CII for T8 cocoindex), T14→ID-42. - §4 retrospective: 6 Tasks (T1-T6) proposed `done` status with provenance journal blocks. PLAN.md is rich with DONE-S2NN annotations making retrospective reconstruction straightforward. T6 ID has deepest evidence (6 commits across 2 sessions). Pre-phase (S180-S241) treated as out-of-scope per OQ-1 default; Liam may override.
- §6 divergence diagnosis: STALE-MERGE only. Three branches use identical custom KH-extended Taskmaster shape (no schema drift) — divergence is purely additive Task additions. No structural duplication, no ID collisions on existing IDs, no schema conflict. Convergence path: next merge cycle reconciles additively.
- §7 tag-model: Option C recommended — separate `docs/reference/umbrellas.json` document with curated `task_ids[]` arrays per umbrella, `substrate_doc` first-class field, supports multi-umbrella membership. Closest fidelity to Linear's Initiative model.

Seven OQs surfaced:
- OQ-1 Retrospective scope — pre-phase (S180-S241)? Default: no.
- OQ-2 Task ID anchor for T8 (keep ID-28 vs renumber)? Default: keep ID-28.
- OQ-3 Forward shape — open all T7-T14 now in pending, or just-in-time? Default: JIT.
- OQ-4 Umbrella schema (Option C vs A vs B)? Default: Option C.
- OQ-5 Cross-branch Task ID coordination (manual discipline vs CI guard)? Default: manual + freshness-guard test.
- OQ-6 Retrospective Subtask granularity (commit-cadence vs session-coarse)? Default: session-coarse.
- OQ-7 PLAN.md ↔ Task description sync (manual cross-doc-links vs auto-sync)? Default: manual cross-doc-links.

Author `lib/validation/umbrellas-schema.ts` per PLAN §2 Subtask 31.5. Mirror the existing pattern in `lib/validation/{task-list,backlog,roadmap}-schema.ts` — strict-mode Zod objects, no barrel re-export, named-export each schema + each inferred type.

Exports required:
- `UmbrellaStatus = z.enum(['proposed', 'in_progress', 'done', 'archived'])`.
- `UmbrellaEntrySchema` with 6 required fields (`id` kebab-case regex; `title` min(1); `substrate_doc` min(1); `task_ids` array of bare-id regex strings; `status` UmbrellaStatus; `phase` min(1)); .strict().
- `UmbrellasSchema` root with 5 fields (`document_name: z.literal('umbrellas')`; `document_purpose` min(1); `last_updated` with 200-char cap + single-line + single-session-id refine + kh-{prod-readiness|main}-S{N} prefix regex; `related_documents: z.array(z.string())`; `umbrellas: z.array(UmbrellaEntrySchema)`); .strict().

Imports: `import { z } from 'zod'`; `import { BARE_ID_REGEX } from '@/lib/validation/schemas'`. No barrel re-export.

FORWARD-COMPAT CONSTRAINT (LOAD-BEARING per Wave 2 merge): The 6 UmbrellaEntry field names — `id`, `title`, `substrate_doc`, `task_ids`, `status`, `phase` — are LOCKED. ID-9 docubot (Wave 7) consumes verbatim. No renames without surfacing as T-OQ.

Verify: `bun run test -- umbrellas-schema` green; `tsc --noEmit` zero new errors.

NO HELPER FILE in this Subtask (lives in 31.6). NO `umbrellas.json` authoring (lives in 31.7).

**Test strategy:** Unit test `__tests__/validation/umbrellas-helpers.test.ts` matches helper output against TECH §5.2 worked example fixture (timestamp-stubbed), verifies opening/closing timestamps align, and verifies optional fields render correctly when present and omit when absent.

**Details:**

Implement `formatRetrospectiveJournalBlock` helper per PLAN §2 Subtask 31.6 + TECH §5.1 signature + §5.2 worked example.

T-OQ-1 RATIFIED DEFAULT: Helper file lives at `lib/validation/umbrellas-helpers.ts` (NOT `lib/ledger/`). Sibling to umbrellas-schema.ts for co-location. Confidence HIGH per WP0 §4.

Exact signature (from TECH §5.1):
```typescript
import type { UmbrellaEntry } from '@/lib/validation/umbrellas-schema';

export interface RetrospectiveOpeningInput {
  retro_open_session: string;
  original_session: string;
  original_branch: string;
  continuation_prompt_path: string;
  commits: Array<{ sha8: string; message_line: string }>;
  migration_files?: string[];
  plan_md_section: string;
  followup_flags?: string[];
  umbrella_id?: string;
}

export function formatRetrospectiveJournalBlock(input: RetrospectiveOpeningInput): string
```

Output template: opens with `<info added on {ISO}>`, contains 'RETROSPECTIVE OPENING — Task opened in `done` status post-implementation.' + Original-work line + Commits block (one line per commit) + optional Migration files block + optional Follow-up flags block + optional Umbrella line + 'PLAN.md §{n} acceptance criteria all met.' closing line + closes with `</info added on {ISO}>` matching opening timestamp.

Generate timestamp via `new Date().toISOString()` at invocation time; tests inject fixed timestamp via `vi.useFakeTimers()` / `vi.setSystemTime()`.

UK English enforced (PRODUCT inv 15). Helper does NOT load umbrellas.json — `umbrella_id` is inserted as plain string; caller verifies id existence.

Unit test cases in `__tests__/validation/umbrellas-helpers.test.ts`: output bounded by opening + closing tags; timestamps match (regex-extracted); body contains required structural lines; optional sections render when input non-empty, omit when undefined/empty; fixture round-trip against TECH §5.2 worked example (timestamp-stubbed).

Verify: `bun run test -- umbrellas-helpers` green; `tsc --noEmit` zero new errors.

No barrel re-export.
